commit.message:
feat: hub-rank scores on illuminate results (1.0=most central → 0)

All illuminate: results previously returned score=1.0 — meaningless to
both humans ('lol all the same') and LLMs (can't prioritize by relevance).

illuminate-global now assigns a normalized hub-rank score:
  score = (N - rank_index) / N
  - score 1.0  → most connected compound in the corpus
  - score ~0   → least connected in the returned set
  - Results sorted descending by score so order matches centrality

Results are also re-sorted by score so the YAML output presents the
most structurally important content first.

UI: score: n.score || 1 → n.score ?? 1 to avoid clobbering a valid
score of 0 for the least-central node.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correctly handle GMS2 tileset borders, tile grid scaling, and global tile dimensions (#6)

* fix(extract): resolve GMS2 compat layer tile tilesets via SpriteDefinition

In GMS2 (Deltarune), tiles in Compatibility_Tiles layers have a null
BackgroundDefinition. The tileset reference is stored in SpriteDefinition
instead, pointing to a UndertaleSprite named 'bg_*'.

Also export all sprites with 'bg_' prefix into backgrounds.json so the
Rust conversion can find their texture data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gms2): use GMS2TileWidth/Height for native tile layer tilesets

GMS2 Background resources store explicit tile dimensions (GMS2TileWidth /
GMS2TileHeight). Previously determine_tile_dims() divided the tileset
texture size by the room's tile count (tiles_x / tiles_y), producing
wrong values (e.g. 12x34 instead of 20x20 for bg_towntiles_tileset).

Changes:
- extract_data.csx: export gms2TileWidth/gms2TileHeight for Data.Backgrounds
- schema.rs: add gms2_tile_width/gms2_tile_height fields to BackgroundDef
- convert.rs: use these fields directly when non-zero, skipping the
  broken tile-count division heuristic

commit.message:
fix: replace dynamic imports with static imports to fix asset pipeline

The webview message handlers in useExtensionMessages.ts used dynamic
import() calls (e.g. import('../office/sprites/assetLoader.js').then(...))
to lazily load asset-management modules. Vite code-splits these into
separate chunks whose module-level state is isolated from the main
bundle. For example, calling setWallSprites on the chunk's copy of
wallTiles.ts had NO effect on the copy used by renderer.ts (imported
statically). This caused ALL three rendering pipelines to silently fail:

1. Character sprite sheets — loadCharacterSheetsFromUris stored sheets
   in the chunk's characterSheets Map, not the renderer's copy
2. Tileset metadata — setTilesetMetadata stored the PNG image in the
   chunk's tilesetMetadataImage, so drawTilesetTile always returned
   false (plain beige/tan floor fallback)
3. Legacy tileset data — setLegacyTilesetAssets wrote to the chunk's
   tilesetData, so drawTilesetFurniture always returned false (gray
   rectangle furniture fallback)

Changes:
- Convert all dynamic import() to static import in useExtensionMessages
- Add try/catch around removeBackground() in character sheet loading
- Add connect-src to CSP so fetch()-based asset loading can work
- Set Vite emptyOutDir: true to prevent stale code-split chunks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scribe: Document CSP race condition diagnostic and fix

Lisa Simpson diagnosed and fixed a critical race condition in webview asset loading:
- User reported F5 testing shows default colored rectangles instead of custom sprites
- Root cause: loadAssets() used fetch() which is blocked by VS Code CSP (no connect-src)
- The catch block reset assetsReady=false, overriding the true already set by URI-based loaders
- Solution: Removed eager loadAssets() call and guarded catch block

Files modified in webview:
- webview-ui/src/office/components/OfficeCanvas.tsx (removed loadAssets call)
- webview-ui/src/office/sprites/assetLoader.ts (guarded catch block)

New Decision § 16: Never use fetch() in webview — CSP blocks it.
All asset loading must use extension-provided URIs via postMessage.

Orchestration log: .squad/orchestration-log/2026-03-08T050537Z-lisa-simpson-csp-race-fix.md

commit.message:
Add scenario dashboard and multi-scenario management for signed-in users (#90)

Signed-in users previously had a single autosaved calculator state with
no way to manage multiple scenarios or navigate between them. This adds
a full scenario management layer: named scenarios, a dashboard to
list/open/rename/delete them, and autosave that captures key inputs and
headline results for dashboard display.

## Storage layer

- `StorageManager` gains `renameScenario()`, `createNewScenario()`,
`activateScenario()`, and `_extractKeyInputs()`
- Caches `activeScenarioId` / `activeScenarioName` to eliminate
redundant profile fetches on every autosave
- `_extractKeyInputs()` extracts `parent1Income`, `parent2Income`,
`childrenCount`, `workDaysCount`, `weeklyOutOfPocket` from the save
state — stored server-side so the dashboard summary requires no extra
fetches
- `saveScenario()` now uses the cached scenario ID and includes
`keyInputs` in the payload; falls back to profile lookup only when the
ID is unknown

```js
// autosave now includes results so out-of-pocket is persisted with the scenario
const state = { formData, results: lastResults, timestamp };
storageManager.autoSave(state);
```

## API (`api/src/services/scenarios.js`)

- `createScenario` / `updateScenario` persist a `keyInputs` JSON column
- `_toScenarioSummary` returns `keyInputs` in list responses — dashboard
reads everything it needs from a single `GET /api/scenarios`

## Dashboard (`src/dashboard.html` + `src/js/ui/dashboard-manager.js`)

- Scenario cards show name, last-updated relative time, key inputs, and
weekly out-of-pocket headline
- Rename modal doubles as the "New Scenario" dialog
(`_isNewScenarioMode` flag) to avoid duplicating modal markup
- All errors are inline (no `alert()`); delete failure shown inside the
confirmation modal

## Calculator integration (`src/index.html` + `src/app.js` +
`src/js/ui/form-handler.js`)

- Auth panel gains: current scenario name, inline rename form, inline
new-scenario form (no `prompt()`), "My Scenarios" link
- `form-handler.js` detects `?scenarioId=` on load, activates the
requested scenario, cleans the URL, and dispatches `scenarioChanged` —

commit.message:
fix: remove eager loadAssets() call that reset assetsReady flag

OfficeCanvas.tsx called loadAssets() on mount, which used fetch()
to load tileset.json via relative URLs. This always failed because
the webview CSP has default-src 'none' with no connect-src directive.
The catch block then set assetsReady = false, overriding the true
value set by URI-based loaders (tilesetMetadataLoaded,
characterAssetsLoaded messages from the extension host).

Fix: Remove the eager loadAssets() call from OfficeCanvas since the
extension host sends webview-safe URIs for all PNG assets. Also guard
the catch block in loadAssets() so it only resets assetsReady if no
URI-based loader has already succeeded.

fix: add missing tg_user_* argtypes/restype in binding.py

Without argtypes=[c_void_p], ctypes truncates the 64-bit pointer to
32 bits when passing user_ptr to tg_user_id/tg_user_first_name etc.
This produced a garbage pointer and caused a segfault in User.__init__.

Added bindings for:
- tg_user_id        (restype=c_int64)
- tg_user_first_name (restype=c_char_p)
- tg_user_last_name  (restype=c_char_p)
- tg_user_username   (restype=c_char_p)
- tg_user_is_bot     (restype=c_int)

commit.message:
Granularize (#23)

* Add granularize() function and GrainCollection iterator

- Add GranularizeOptions interface and granularize() method to DatabaseManager
  (database.ts): computes grain start positions, stores as 'granularize'
  feature, creates derived samples for non-silent grains via existing
  createDerivedSample path
- Extract private computeFeatureHash() helper from storeFeature() so the
  feature hash can be reused without duplication
- Add granularize-sample IPC handler (main.ts)
- Expose granularizeSample via contextBridge (preload.ts, types.d.ts)
- Implement GrainCollection class (src/renderer/grain-collection.ts) with
  length(), forEach() (sequential async), map(), filter(), toString()
- Add granularize() to BounceApi (bounce-api.ts): resolves source same as
  play()/slice(), builds GrainCollection from returned grain hashes
- Update bounce-globals.d.ts with GranularizeOptions, GrainCollection,
  and granularize() declaration
- Add grain-collection unit tests (src/grain-collection.test.ts)
- Add granularize Playwright E2E test (tests/granularize.spec.ts)

Options: grainSize (ms, default 20), hopSize, jitter, startTime, endTime,
normalize, silenceThreshold (dBFS, default -60). Hard limit: 20s source.
Grains stored as raw PCM; windowing is a playback-time concern.

commit.message:
fix(c): implement proper HTML/Markdown formatting via parseTextEntities

Previously parse_mode was put inside inputMessageText as an unknown field
(TDLib ignores it), so all formatting was silently dropped.

Changes:
- tg_internal.h: add TG_FMTTEXT_MAX = TG_TEXT_MAX*6 for formatted text buffers
- tg_message.c: add _json_str_escape() to safely embed text in JSON
- tg_message.c: rewrite _build_text_content() to call td_execute() with
  parseTextEntities (documented as synchronous in TDLib) for html/markdown
  modes; the returned formattedText (with entities array) is embedded
  directly in inputMessageText — identical to how Pyrogram/Telethon work
- tg_message.c: update tg_send_text_ex/tg_edit_text_ex to use
  _build_text_content() instead of duplicating the broken logic
- tg_message.c: increase all send/edit buffers from TG_TEXT_MAX to
  TG_FMTTEXT_MAX to accommodate entity-rich responses
- Rebuilt libtg.so

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(c,client): fix segfault race + rename msg_id params for API consistency

- tg_internal.h: add dispatch_count field to tg_client_t to track
  in-flight C callbacks
- tg_client.c: increment dispatch_count under run_lock before dispatch,
  decrement + broadcast after; tg_client_stop() waits for count to reach
  zero — prevents use-after-free when tg_client_free() is called while
  the poll thread is still inside _tg_dispatch()
- client.py: rename msg_id→message_id, msg_ids→message_ids in
  edit_message_text, delete_messages, copy_message, forward_messages,
  pin_message, unpin_message, edit_message, delete_message, get_messages
  to match what TelegramContext passes as kwargs

  aliases for Pyrogram-compat context calls

commit.message:
Scan deeper candidate windows for distributed claims

When many oldest rows are temporarily ineligible (for example faces waiting
on objects), a small claim window can return empty and leave workers polling
idle despite remaining backlog. Expand jobs/claim to scan multiple bounded
candidate windows in one request, and add a regression test that ensures an
eligible newer faces job is still leased.
